title: Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Stefon Diggs remains a free agent after being released by the Patriots to free up cap space in March.
Despite his 1,045-yard season, Patriots writer Phil Perry doubts a Diggs reunion given possible A.J. Brown trade.
Diggs' court acquittal and prior influences in 2025 led to the Patriots seeking younger and more secure options.

title: Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Coan, 31, died from gunshot wounds inside his car in New Bedford, Bristol County DA reports.
ShotSpotter detected gunshots near Acushnet Avenue and Russell Street on May 6 at 11:30 p.m.
The shooting is under investigation as a homicide by State Police and New Bedford police.

title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kevin Hayden, Linda Champion, and Rachael Rollins have gathered enough signatures to qualify for the Suffolk DA primary.
Rachael Rollins, a former Suffolk DA and U.S. Attorney, resigned amid ethics violations in 2023 and is making a comeback.
Rollins has $42,000, Champion almost $39,000, and Hayden over $60,000 in campaign funds for the primary on Sept. 1.

title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair CEO Niraj Shah topped Boston's highest-paid tech CEOs list with $280.6 million in stock awards.
Analog Devices CEO Vincent Roche and PTC Inc. CEO Neil Barua ranked second and third with $27.5M and $24.3M, respectively.
The BBJ report ranked tech CEOs based on total compensation, including salary, bonuses, and stock options.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Karen Read's 2021 Lexus LX 570 is set for auction in Woburn on May 14 at Manzi Appraisers.
Readers estimate the SUV could sell for less than $70,000 to over $500,000 due to its notoriety and condition.
The auction requires bidders to register with a valid ID and $20,000 in bank funds; a 10% buyer's premium applies.

title: Shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge leaves ‘at least’ one wounded, police say
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A police-involved shooting occurred on Memorial Drive, Cambridge, around 1:30 p.m., leaving at least one person wounded.
The suspect is in custody, and there is no ongoing threat to Harvard University or the general public.
Cambridge police advised residents to avoid the area as the investigation into the shooting continues.

title: Police investigating viral video of apparent food contamination at McDonald’s in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Southbridge police are investigating a viral video of a McDonald's worker allegedly contaminating food by eating and repackaging fries.
The franchise owners stated that involved individuals have left McDonald's and confirmed no health violations were found.
Local authorities and health officials are collaborating to determine if the tainted food was served to customers.

title: MassINC poll: Many Bay Staters say their quality of life is slipping in Mass.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A MassINC poll shows 40% of Massachusetts residents feel their quality of life has worsened over the past five years.
More than half of respondents say housing is unaffordable, with 65% among ages 35-44 struggling with costs.
Only 27% expect financial improvement next year, highlighting concerns over economic stability and rising living costs.

title: Celtics reportedly tried to land Giannis Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics reportedly attempted to trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo at February's deadline, along with the Lakers, Knicks, Timberwolves, and Cavaliers.
Antetokounmpo, a two-time MVP, is open to trade discussions as the Bucks field offers following a series of playoff setbacks.
Acquiring Antetokounmpo would require substantial salary moves by the Celtics, who struggled with points in the paint last season.

title: Student charged in Danvers home invasion murder appears in court
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, charged with murder and home invasion, pleaded not guilty in a Danvers case involving Janet Swallow's death.
DeMayo is held without bail and deemed incompetent to stand trial, receiving treatment at Bridgewater State Hospital.
A court-approved independent competency evaluation was requested by prosecutors in addition to Bridgewater's existing assessment.
